page: Vultr vhf-1c-2gb is a virtual machine instance within the High Frequency Compute family. It features 1 shared vCPU based on the Intel x86_64 architecture, with 1 physical core and 1 thread per core. The instance is equipped with 2.0 GB of system memory, providing a memory-to-core ratio of 2.0 GB per core.

For local storage, the instance includes a bundled 64 GB NVMe SSD. It also comes provisioned with 1 complimentary public IPv4 address. There is no dedicated GPU hardware associated with this server type.

This server type offers a highly cost-effective entry point for workloads that do not require dedicated CPU resources. Because the CPU allocation is shared, performance may vary depending on host node utilization. The inclusion of high-speed NVMe SSD storage provides a qualitative performance advantage for disk-bound operations compared to standard block storage, despite the entry-level compute profile.

The Vultr vhf-1c-2gb is designed for lightweight applications, development environments, small-scale web serving, low-traffic databases, and basic utility scripting where a single shared vCPU and 2.0 GB of RAM are sufficient.
description: Vultr vhf-1c-2gb is an entry-level virtual machine in the High Frequency Compute family, featuring a single shared Intel x86_64 vCPU with one physical core and one thread. It is configured with 2.0 GB of system memory and includes 64 GB of local NVMe SSD storage, alongside one complimentary public IPv4 address. This instance does not include a GPU. Designed as a budget-friendly option, it utilizes a shared CPU allocation, making it highly cost-efficient for workloads with low CPU utilization. The bundled NVMe SSD storage enhances read and write speeds for local data access. This server type is best suited for lightweight workloads, including small web servers, development environments, utility scripts, and low-traffic databases.

Suited for lightweight web serving, development, and basic scripting
